description: Make `springfield ralph run` execute real Claude or Codex commands through a shared runtime boundary and persist truthful run records.

userStories.title: Build Claude and Codex command adapters behind a shared runtime contract
userStories.description: Write failing tests first, then extend `internal/core/agents/` from detect-only adapters to command-spec adapters for `claude` and `codex`, and add the initial shared runtime API under `internal/core/runtime/` for prompt, working directory, timeout, and event streaming.
acceptanceCriteria: The public agent boundary can resolve a runnable command spec for the project default agent.
The first real runtime slice supports `claude` and `codex` only.
The runtime surface can stream structured events suitable for both CLI and later TUI consumers.
Tests cover command construction and runtime success and failure paths.
userStories.notes: Use project default agent from `springfield.toml`; do not add per-plan override in this cycle. Keep `gemini` as doctor-only detection for now.

userStories.title: Replace the Ralph noop executor with a real runner
userStories.description: Implement `internal/features/ralph/runner.go` and wire `cmd/ralph.go` so `springfield ralph run` uses the shared runtime instead of `noopStoryExecutor`, choosing the next eligible story and executing it with the resolved project default agent.
acceptanceCriteria: `springfield ralph run` no longer succeeds via a noop path.
Ralph execution reuses the shared runtime instead of duplicating process code in the command layer.
The command layer in `cmd/ralph.go` stays thin and feature logic lives under `internal/features/ralph/`.
Tests cover the happy path and execution failure path for `springfield ralph run`.
userStories.notes: Current placeholder seam is `cmd/ralph.go` `noopStoryExecutor`. Preserve current project-local `.springfield/` state layout unless the approved plan explicitly changes it.

userStories.title: Persist truthful Ralph run evidence and surface failure details
userStories.description: Persist run result details that reflect real execution, including chosen agent, timestamps, terminal status, and failure text, and update tests so the stored records and CLI output do not claim success when the runtime failed.
acceptanceCriteria: Persisted Ralph run records include truthful status, timestamps, agent, and error detail.
Failure paths preserve enough evidence for later diagnosis.
CLI output for `springfield ralph run` reflects real success or failure rather than placeholder completion.
userStories.notes: Keep this slice focused on real runtime truthfulness, not later TUI polish.
